message:
feat(widgets/inspector): selection-first detail panel with hit-region breakdown and render-time readout

Reworks the inspector overlay's detail panel so it prioritises whatever
widget the user has selected, falls back to hover info, and finally
shows an empty-state message. Along the way it fixes a subtle
warning-banner clipping bug and adds the plumbing needed to show
per-widget render time.

WidgetInfo — new API surface (no behavioural changes to the struct's
existing fields)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
* `with_render_time_us(render_time_us: u64) -> Self` — builder method
  to attach measured render time. Stored in `render_time_us: Option<u64>`
  (already present on the struct) and must be set by producers that
  want the overlay to show the readout.
* `find_by_hit_id(id: HitId) -> Option<&Self>` — recursive tree walk
  matching on `self.hit_id == Some(id)`, used by the overlay to
  resolve `state.selected` back to a `WidgetInfo` without touching
  private fields.
* `region_counts() -> Vec<(String, usize)>` and the private
  `accumulate_region_counts` helper — walk the subtree and produce a
  deduplicated `(region_debug_name, count)` list for the detail panel.
  Uses a `Vec` instead of a `HashMap` to preserve insertion order so
  the display is stable across frames.

InspectorOverlay — detail panel rewrite
---------------------------------------
* New `selected_widget(&self) -> Option<&WidgetInfo>` looks up the
  currently selected hit id through all top-level `state.widgets` via
  `find_by_hit_id`.
* The detail panel is now structured as three fallthrough branches:
    1. If `selected_widget()` resolves, call
       `draw_selected_widget_details` and return.
    2. Otherwise, if `draw_hover_details` returns true (i.e. the user
       is hovering over something), render the hover readout.
    3. Otherwise show "Selected: <id> / Widget missing" when there is
       a dangling selection, or "No widgets" / "No selection"
       depending on whether the overlay has any widgets at all.
* `draw_selected_widget_details` renders:
    - `Widget: <name>` (or `<unnamed>`)
    - `ID: <hit_id>`
    - An `Area:` block with x / y / w / h on separate lines
    - A `Hit Regions:` block listing `<count> <region_debug_name>`
      entries via `widget.region_counts()`
    - A `Render: <us>us` readout, gated on `state.show_times` and
      the widget having `render_time_us` populated.
* `draw_hover_details` is the old hover-rendering code extracted into
  a helper that takes `y: &mut u16` and returns `bool`. The existing
  hover-id lookup now additionally resolves the hovered `WidgetInfo`
  through `find_by_hit_id` and, when `state.show_times` is on,
  renders the same `Render: <us>us` line underneath the ID.

Warning banner — clipping fix
-----------------------------
Previously the warning banner centered itself inside the raw `area`
Rect and passed `area.x + area.width` as the text span's right bound.
When `area` extended past the frame buffer bounds (shrinking terminal,
detached overlay anchors), the banner could draw into invalid cells.

The new flow:
  1. `let clip = area.intersection(&frame.buffer.bounds())` and
     bail out early if `clip.is_empty()`.
  2. Re-center inside `clip`, compute `warning_width = msg_len.min(clip.right().saturating_sub(x))`,
     bail if that goes to zero.
  3. Style and draw over `Rect::new(x, y, warning_width, 1)` with
     `clip.right()` as the `draw_text_span` right bound.

This keeps the banner visible in the widest column that actually fits
and never issues out-of-bounds writes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
